new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes, including fortified wines (wines to which alcohol has been added), classifiable under CN heading 2204. Excludes grape must of heading 2009.
Wine of fresh grapes to which ethyl alcohol or grape spirit has been added to arrest fermentation or raise the final alcoholic strength, as referred to in CN heading 2204.
Unfermented or partially fermented grape must classifiable under CN heading 2204, excluding grape juice or must of heading 2009.
The fermentation process applied to fresh grapes or grape must that produces wine of fresh grapes under CN heading 2204.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main commodity categories of heading 2204: (1) wine of fresh grapes, (2) fortified wine as a subclass thereof, and (3) grape must under 2204. The production process is linked via eucn:producedBy. The exclusion of grape must of heading 2009 (grape juice) is noted in the definition text but cannot be fully expressed as a formal OWL complement restriction without a class for heading 2009 grape must present in the TBox. The reference to Additional Note 2(a) on 'actual alcoholic strength by volume' is a cross-reference to an interpretive note and does not itself introduce a new distinguishing criterion beyond the existing eucn:alcoholByVolumePercent property already in the TBox — this is not additionally axiomatised here to avoid over-commitment. Coverage is reduced accordingly for the missing 2009 exclusion restriction and the unreferenced additional note linkage.
